examples.title: Runtime Polymorphism with Virtual Functions
examples.description: Demonstrates runtime polymorphism using base class pointer and virtual functions
examples.explanation: This program demonstrates runtime polymorphism by using base class pointers to derived class objects. The virtual sound() function is resolved at runtime based on the actual object type (Dog or Cat), not the pointer type.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) per function call, O(n) for n objects
examples.spaceComplexity: O(1) per object + O(n) for array of n pointers

examples.title: Abstract Class with Pure Virtual Functions
examples.description: Demonstrates abstract classes and pure virtual functions
examples.explanation: Shape is an abstract class with pure virtual function area(). Derived classes Rectangle and Circle must implement area() to become concrete. Polymorphism allows calling area() through base class pointer to get correct derived class implementation.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for area calculation per shape type

examples.title: Virtual Destructor Demonstration
examples.description: Shows importance of virtual destructor for proper cleanup
examples.explanation: This demonstrates why virtual destructors are essential. When deleting through base pointer, virtual destructor ensures derived class destructor is called first (freeing derived-allocated memory), then base destructor. Without virtual, only base destructor runs causing memory leaks.
examples.spaceComplexity: O(n) for dynamically allocated memory in Derived

examples.title: Python Equivalent - Duck Typing
examples.description: Demonstrates Python's approach to polymorphism (duck typing)
examples.explanation: Python uses duck typing ('if it walks like a duck...') for polymorphism. No need for explicit virtual functions or base class pointers - any object with sound() method works. This is different from C++ compile-time vtable approach.
